subject: Cloud Computing
title: VMware and the Virtual Machine Boom
description: A 60-second lesson on VMware and the Virtual Machine Boom.

hook.text: In 1998, a Stanford professor and her students figured out how to virtualize Intel processors.

buildup.text: VMware made it possible to run multiple operating systems on one ordinary PC.

discovery.text: Businesses consolidated dozens of servers into a few, saving millions on hardware.

twist.text: VMware became so dominant that it controlled enterprise computing for over a decade.

climax.text: At its peak, VMware powered most data centers worldwide. Then containers arrived.

punchline.text: VMware made virtual machines mainstream. Then the game changed.

quiz.question: What was VMware's key contribution to computing?
options: Making virtualization practical on standard Intel hardware
